page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at 3.1 GHz. The CPU includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes a 300 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates average multi-core CPU performance, average memory bandwidth, and average Passmark database operations. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core results. Decompression tasks and static web serving throughput also fall into the lower performance tier. For machine learning workloads, the hardware achieves average prompt processing and text generation speeds on small 135M models, but drops to poor performance on medium 7B models.

The m5dn.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring local NVMe storage and moderate network throughput. Its qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the dedicated Intel Xeon cores and integrated high-speed local storage, which reduces the need for external network-attached storage for temporary data. This instance is suitable for general-purpose applications, database operations, software compilation, and redis caching, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a 300 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU performance, average memory bandwidth, and average database operations, though single-core performance and static web serving throughput are weak. The instance provides a balanced resource profile, making it suitable for database workloads, software compilation, and caching applications that benefit from local NVMe storage, while showing limitations in single-threaded tasks and larger machine learning models.
